items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Rated load is clearly marked on both sides of crane bridge (Arabic & English)
Rated load is clearly marked on hoist or trolley unit
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane manufacturer name, address, serial number and power ratings are clearly marked or tagged
Precautionary warnings to operator are clearly marked
Clearance exits between the crane and sides of the building or adjacent crane are maintained throughout all motions
Controls and protective equipment are within reach of the operator inside the cab
The hook block is visible from operator station at all times
Cab is attached to the crane to minimize swaying and vibrations
Access to the cab or bridge walkway is by a fixed ladder, stairs, or platform
The clearance from the surface of the platform to the nearest overhead obstruction is 1220 mm (48")
The service platform width is at least 457 mm (18") except at the bridge mechanism where it is not less than 380 mm (15")
The electrical control cabinet door(s) are opening 90 degree or removable type
Service platform walking surface is slip-resistant
Service platform is provided with guard railings and toeboards
Emergency escape is possible from the cab
Stairways are non-slip and have a maximum incline angle of 50 degree
Each hoisting unit is equipped with at least one holding brake
The holding brake is applied to the motor shaft or a gear reducer shaft
The holding brake torque rating is not less than the percentage of rated load hoisting torque at the point where the brake is applied (based on the crane design)
Pendant control cable is properly enclosed, grounded and suspended with a separate support cable
Pendant control push-button enclosure is marked for identification of functions
Electrical equipment is guarded and not exposed to oil, moisture, dirt and inadvertent contact
Audio warning device(s) are fitted (one or more of the following: Gong, Bell/Siren/Horn, Rotating Beacon and/or strope light)
Lifting and lowering functional test is satisfactory
Crane trolley travel functional test is satisfactory
Crane bridge travel functional test is satisfactory
Hoist limit device functional test is satisfactory
Crane Travel limit device functional test is satisfactory
Wire rope end connections do not have corrosion
Ropes are correctly lubricated

Removal criteria for rope replacement shall be as follows: • In running ropes, 12 randomly broken wires in 1 lay or 4 broken wires in 1 strand of 1 lay. • 1 broken wire protruding from the core • Reductions from nominal rope diameter greater than 5% • Kinking, crushing, birdcaging or other distortion.
Wire rope is not corroded
The rope is adequately lubricated
A wind speed indicating device is provided for cranes used outdoors
Fire extinguisher installed in the cab.(10BC minimum rated)
Hoist rope is guarded from chafing where required
Hook(s) can rotate freely
Sections in contact with saddles, equalizer sheaves, or other sheaves where rope travel is limited are not rapid deterioration.
Surface condition of rope drum(s) show no defects and are smooth
Sheave grooves shall be free from surface defects which could cause rope damage
Sheaves carrying ropes, which can be momentarily unloaded, are provided with close-fitting guards, or other devices, to guide the rope back into the groove when the load is reapplied.
Rope construction is as per manufacturer recommendations
Lower hoist limit cut-out (if fitted) is properly working
Stops and bumpers are fitted to each end of the trolley(s)
Trolley truck rail sweeps are provided in front of the leading wheels on both ends of the trolley end truck
Clearance between the top surface of the rail head and the bottom of the sweep does not exceed 3Ú16" (5 mm)
The sweep extends below the top surface of the rail head, for a distance not less than 50% of the thickness of the rail head, on both sides of the rail head
Clearance between the side surface of the rail head and the side of the sweep which extends below the top surface of the rail head is equal to crane float plus 3Ú16" (5 mm)
Trolley(s) brakes are operable
Trolley unit of the crane equipped with either a braking means or have trolley drive frictional characteristics
Trolley travel warnings (e.g. gong, beacon, bell or strope light) are operable
Unusual sounds are not present during trolley travel
Trolley has no missing or loose parts
Trolley wheels have no sign of excessive wear
Chain drive and sprocket have no wear or stretch
All moving parts are correctly lubricated
Crane Bridge stops within stipulated 10% distance of rated load speed under frictional forces (if no braking means provided)
Bridge brakes comply with crane design requirements
Trolley truck frame drop is limited to 25mm
Bridge is fitted with bumpers at each end
Bridge rail sweep clearance is 5mm
Bridge brakes capable of stopping the crane within 10% distance of rated load speed
Outdoor crane is provided with secure fastenings.
Rails used for anchorages are secured to withstand the resultant forces applied by the rail clamps.
The runway structure is free from detrimental vibration under normal operating conditions
Rails are level, straight, joined, and spaced to the crane span within tolerances as per crane design
Runway stops are provided at the limits of travel of the bridge
Stops are designed to withstand the forces applied to the bumpers
Crane is clear from obstruction throughout its travel (between building walls and other cranes)
All moving parts are guarded where potential hazard would exist otherwise
Travel warnings are operational (gong, bell, siren, horn, beacon, or strope light)
Crane structure shows no deformed, cracked or corroded members
All travel limit devices are functioning
Safety labels are displayed and legible
Integral outside platform is in place and door opens outward or slides
Trapdoor has a clear opening of not less than 610mm
Guard railings and toe boards are in good condition
All cab glazing are safety glazing materials
A tool box is in place for basic maintenance made of noncombustible material and is securely fastened in the cab or on the service platform.
Lighting is adequate inside the cab and operator can clearly observe the controls
Means of emergency exit are in place and effective
Control circuit voltage does not exceed 600 volts (AC or DC)
Welded structures and members do not have cracks or corrosion
Adequate clearances exists between two parallel crane bridges (if there are no intervening walls or structures)
Minimum working space on service platforms is 1220mm (48")
Minimum passageway on service platform is 457mm (18")
Doors of electrical cabinets to open 90 degrees or be removable
The crane controllers are equipped with spring return master switches
The resistors are supported and has minimum vibration effects
Runway conductors are guarded
A separate magnet circuit switch of enclosed type is provided (if a lifting magnet is used)
Service receptacle in the cab or on the bridge is grounded type and does not exceed 300 volts (if provided)
The control circuit voltage in pendant push buttons does not exceed 150V for AC or 300V for DC
A suspended push-button station is supported so that the electrical conductors are protected from strain (where multiple conductor cable is used)
Pendant control stations is constructed to prevent electrical shock
The push-button enclosure is at ground potential and marked for identification of functions
Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible
Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1Ú4 in. (6 mm) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook)
Inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning latch that does not close the hook’s throat
Hook is free to rotate
